text.title: Risk Management Antipatterns and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Common risk register failures, misuse of response strategies, and agent-specific pitfalls in automated risk identification and scoring.

antipattern.description: Stale risk register never reviewed after creation.
antipattern.reason: A 6-month-old register is worse than none because it creates false confidence. Risks have resolved, new risks have emerged, and the register now misleads rather than informs. Weekly 15-minute reviews are mandatory.

antipattern.description: Using "Accept" response without a contingency budget line.
antipattern.reason: "Accept" is the most-abused response: it lets teams skip planning while pretending to manage the risk. Accepting without provisioning a reserve is hoping. Every Accept must map to a specific contingency amount equal to its EMV.

antipattern.description: Double-tracking materialized risks as both a Risk and an Issue.
antipattern.reason: When a risk materializes, convert it to an Issue and remove it from the register. Keeping it in both places inflates the risk count and confuses status reporting.

antipattern.description: Setting risk reviews as 2-hour quarterly events instead of 15-minute weekly sessions.
antipattern.reason: Quarterly reviews find risks that have already become crises. Weekly 15-minute reviews catch drift while corrective action is still affordable.

rule.statement: Require each agent-identified risk to cite a specific quoted source from inputs; reject generic risks without evidence.
rule.rationale: LLMs generate generic risks ("scope creep", "team turnover") without grounding in project specifics. Requiring a source quote forces actual analysis of the charter, WBS, and vendor list.

list.item: Probability ratings cluster around "Medium" — too safe; require justification per rating ("M because vendor missed last 2 SLAs in Q1")
Numerical EMV implies false precision; always print confidence interval or skip dollar values in agent output if inputs lack dollar-denominated impact estimates
Agent may merge distinct risks into one (loses owner clarity) or over-split one into many (inflates register); constrain via target count (8-15 risks for a 6-month project)
Auto-closing risks based on absence of mention in status reports is wrong — no-mention is not evidence of resolution; require an explicit "closed by [person] on [date]" field
Do not let an agent tag a risk as Accept without a budget/contingency line item — it must propose a specific dollar amount or token-effort equivalent
Pair risk identification with Change Control: any approved change request triggers a risk re-rating pass
